message:
perf(pager): cache base change counter across WAL probe when generation stable

`PagerInner::probe_visible_commit_seq` is called on the read path to
compute the visible `CommitSeq` without paying for full page-1
materialization or freelist reconstruction when nothing actually
changed. Previously, even on the "nothing changed" fast path, it still
re-read the database file's header bytes from disk each call to
extract the change counter — and that's a non-trivial cost in WAL
mode, where the *only* thing the change counter is doing is providing
a stable base to add `wal_visible_commit_count` to.

Optimization: cache the base change counter on `PagerInner` and only
re-read it when the cache is known stale.

Two new fields:

    committed_db_change_counter: u64
    committed_wal_generation: Option<WalGenerationIdentity>

`committed_db_change_counter` records the last value extracted from
the main DB file header. `committed_wal_generation` records the WAL
generation that was active when we extracted it — when the generation
changes (checkpoint, rollback, schema migration, etc.) the main DB
header may have been rewritten and our cached value is no longer
valid.

The probe now does:

1. Read the WAL pinned snapshot if WAL mode is enabled. Use the
   snapshot's `commit_count` and `generation` directly when present
   (avoids `committed_txn_count`, which involves a heavier path),
   else fall back to `committed_txn_count(cx)`.
2. Compute `cached_wal_base_is_current` =
      WAL mode
      AND  file size unchanged since last commit
      AND  WAL generation present and equal to cached.
3. If cached, reuse `committed_db_change_counter` directly. Otherwise
   fall through to the existing read-and-parse-header path and update
   the two cache fields with the freshly-read counter and current
   generation.

The else branch (rollback-journal mode, or stale cache) keeps the
exact same logic that was there before — read header bytes, decode
header, fall back to `stale_main_header_change_counter_under_wal`
under WAL recovery races. No semantic change in any of those paths;
they just now also write back the cache before returning.

A few mechanical details worth flagging:

- `probe_visible_commit_seq` had to take `&mut self` (was `&self`)
  because we now mutate the cache fields. The single existing caller
  already had `&mut PagerInner` available.
- Both `PagerInner` constructors (the standard open path around line
  5497 and the recovery/header-might-be-missing path around line
  5679) seed `committed_db_change_counter` from the initial header
  read so the first call to `probe_visible_commit_seq` already has a
  valid cache when it computes `cached_wal_base_is_current` (file
  size matches, generation is `None` on both sides until the first
  WAL snapshot, so the cache is technically not used on call #1, but
  the value is immediately correct for call #2 onward).
- The existing `WalGenerationIdentity` import had to be added
  (re-exported alongside the other `fsqlite_wal::...` items the
  file already pulls in).
- One unrelated whitespace move: the error string
  `"invalid database header during pager refresh: {error}"` for the
  non-WAL else branch is unchanged in content; only its formatting
  shifted because of the surrounding indentation change. Kept that
  drift in this commit so the diff is self-contained.

Steady-state effect: in WAL mode under read-mostly workloads, the
visible-commit-seq probe avoids one disk read per call (a single
header-sized read, but it's on the read path of every snapshot
refresh). Behavior is unchanged whenever the WAL generation rolls
over — the cache is invalidated and the slow path runs as before.
